Documentation.long_help: Interactive 3D ruler on a vtkDataSet. Drag the two endpoint handles; on release each endpoint is snapped with a screen-ray pick to the nearest visible mesh point (ParaView Select Points / ConvertDisplayToPointOnSurface). Computes the shortest path along triangle mesh edges or, for line-only inputs, along VTK_LINE / VTK_POLY_LINE segments (Dijkstra), and outputs the path polyline.
Documentation.short_help: Ruler with screen-ray vertex snap and surface or polyline path distance.

InputProperty.name: Input
InputProperty.Documentation: Surface or polyline vtkDataSet. Path uses triangle mesh edges when polygons are present; otherwise walks line / poly-line segments between input points.

DoubleVectorProperty.name: Point1
DoubleVectorProperty.label: Point 1
DoubleVectorProperty.Documentation: World position of endpoint 1 (snapped to nearest input vertex when enabled).

DoubleVectorProperty.name: Point2
DoubleVectorProperty.label: Point 2
DoubleVectorProperty.Documentation: World position of endpoint 2 (snapped to nearest input vertex when enabled).

IntVectorProperty.name: Point1VertexId
IntVectorProperty.label: Point 1 vertex id
IntVectorProperty.Documentation: Input point id for endpoint 1 (default 0 = minimum id).

IntVectorProperty.name: Point2VertexId
IntVectorProperty.label: Point 2 vertex id
IntVectorProperty.Documentation: Input point id for endpoint 2. Default -1 auto-initializes to the maximum id (last point) on the first update after Input is connected.

IntVectorProperty.name: SnapToInputVertices
IntVectorProperty.label: Snap to input vertices
IntVectorProperty.Documentation: When on (default) and vertex ids are out of range, endpoints are re-snapped from world positions. When Point1VertexId / Point2VertexId are valid (typical after interactive pick and Apply), those ids are used directly for the geodesic output.

DoubleVectorProperty.name: GeodesicDistance
DoubleVectorProperty.label: Geodesic distance
DoubleVectorProperty.Documentation: Shortest path distance along the surface mesh or polyline graph (-1 if unreachable).
